title: ranunculus bulbosus subsp. castellanus
intro: Ranunculus bulbosus subsp. castellanus is a subspecies of the lesser celandine plant, a perennial plant native to the Iberian Peninsula, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects, which have been attributed to the presence of alkaloids and other compounds. The health benefits of Ranunculus bulbosus subsp. castellanus include its potential use in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken internally to leverage its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
